title: Q2 household budget forecast
summary: Looking at the next three months: groceries trending 8% over, daycare jumps in May, and the car insurance renewal hits June.
body_html:
Q2 budget forecast

Pulling together what I see in the spending data through end of March against the household budget you set in January. Three things I want to flag, in order of how soon they'll bite.

Groceries are running 8% over plan

Plan was $1,200/month; actual through Q1 averaged $1,295. The biggest line item is the weekly farmers market run, which is up about $30/week compared to last year. Some of that is the new asparagus-and-strawberry combo Rachel started buying, some is the eggs going from $6 to $8 a dozen.

Not catastrophic — under $100/month is well inside the contingency line — but worth knowing.

Daycare jumps May 1

Pickle moves from the toddler room ($1,400/mo) to the preschool room ($1,650/mo). That's +$250/mo starting May 1, which the budget already accounts for, but worth a calendar reminder so it doesn't surprise anyone in the May statement.

Car insurance renewal June 12

The renewal quote came in at $2,180 for six months, up from $1,890. That's a 15% increase with no claims and no driving record changes. Worth shopping it before you accept — I'd start with State Farm and Progressive for comparison quotes; they're typically 10-20% below the renewal price.

Net effect on Q2

If everything lands as expected and we don't shop the insurance:

MonthPlanForecastDelta
April$5,400$5,495+$95
May$5,400$5,745+$345
June$5,400$5,995+$595

Cumulatively about $1,035 over plan for the quarter, almost all of which is the daycare bump and the insurance renewal. Shopping the insurance to Progressive at the typical discount would close roughly half the gap.

Want me to draft the insurance quote request emails? I can pre-fill the policy details from the family vault and you'd just need to send them.
